title: podman cli does not match docker cli when no sub command is provided
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
When one runs the podman cli without a subcommand podman prints an error message and returns a non zero exit code.

When one runs the docker cli without a subcommand docker prints a help message and returns zero on the command line.

My understanding is that the podman cli should be a drop in replacement for the docker cli and this is a case where they differ. Can the podman cli please match the behavior of the docker cli?

If this is fixed can it also be backported or cherry picked to the podman available in the CentOS 7 repos?
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. On CentOS 7 run `sudo podman`

2. Install the latest docker cli from the docker repos https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/centos/

3. On the same CentOS 7 machine run `sudo docker`

**Describe the results you received:**
$ sudo podman
Error: missing command 'podman COMMAND'
Try 'podman --help' for more information.
$ echo $?
125

$ sudo docker
Usage:  docker [OPTIONS] COMMAND

A self-sufficient runtime for containers

Options:
      --config string      Location of client config files (default "/root/.docker")
  -c, --context string     Name of the context to use to connect to the daemon (overrides DOCKER_HOST env var and default context set with "docker context use")
  -D, --debug              Enable debug mode
  -H, --host list          Daemon socket(s) to connect to
  -l, --log-level string   Set the logging level ("debug"|"info"|"warn"|"error"|"fatal") (default "info")
      --tls                Use TLS; implied by --tlsverify
      --tlscacert string   Trust certs signed only by this CA (default "/root/.docker/ca.pem")
      --tlscert string     Path to TLS certificate file (default "/root/.docker/cert.pem")
      --tlskey string      Path to TLS key file (default "/root/.docker/key.pem")
      --tlsverify          Use TLS and verify the remote
  -v, --version            Print version information and quit

Management Commands:
  builder     Manage builds
  config      Manage Docker configs
  container   Manage containers
  context     Manage contexts
  engine      Manage the docker engine
  image       Manage images
...
$ echo $?
0

**Describe the results you expected:**
Both commands should have similar output and they should both return zero on the command line

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.4.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.3
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.10.3
  podman version: 1.4.4
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.0
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.4.4-2.el7.centos.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 0.3.0, commit: unknown'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"centos"'
    version: "7"
  MemFree: 1708429312
  MemTotal: 3936452608
  OCIRuntime:
    package: containerd.io-1.2.13-3.2.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc10
      commit: dc9208a3303feef5b3839f4323d9beb36df0a9dd
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 4294963200
  SwapTotal: 4294963200
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 2
  hostname: gla1vm201
  kernel: 3.10.0-957.27.2.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 3h 6m 22.88s (Approximately 0.12 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 0
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.4.4-2.el7.centos.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Virtual Machine

comments.body:
I disagree.  I don't want podman --help to pop up on every execution of a command.  Most commands work like podman 
```
$ ls --foobar
ls: unrecognized option '--foobar'
Try 'ls --help' for more information.
$ cut
cut: you must specify a list of bytes, characters, or fields
Try 'cut --help' for more information.
$ echo $?
1
$ grep
Usage: grep [OPTION]... PATTERNS [FILE]...
Try 'grep --help' for more information.
$ echo $?
2
```
We do not match Docker when it is a what we consider a bug.
If you type into the screen, 
```
$ docker --foobar 2>&1 | wc -l
84
``` 
Docker dumps 84 lines worth of text and the actual error message scrolls off of the terminal. Most of this useless information that you probably already know. Now you have to scroll up to attempt to figure out what the mistake was. If you don't know how to use the command line, then you would issue  a docker --help.  

As opposed to
```
$ podman --foobar
Error: unknown flag: --foobar
```
